description_long: This object is a vintage twin-lens reflex camera, specifically a Yashica-Mat LM, featuring a robust rectangular body with a matte black finish and metallic accents. The front face displays two prominent circular lenses stacked vertically, with the upper lens serving as the viewing lens and the lower as the taking lens. A metallic dial with colored markings and a lever is visible on one side, likely for adjusting film speed or aperture, while the opposite side features a large knurled knob for film advance and another dial for settings. The top of the camera has a flat, square panel with subtle etched lines, indicating the location of the viewfinder hood, which can be flipped open for waist-level viewing. The camera's exterior is primarily metal with some plastic or bakelite components, and the overall design is boxy and utilitarian, typical of mid-20th-century photographic equipment. Branding is visible on the front, with the name 'Yashica-Mat' and 'LM' clearly marked.
description: A vintage Yashica-Mat LM twin-lens reflex camera with a boxy black metal body, dual front lenses, and prominent dials and branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing a large metallic dial with colored markings and a lever, as well as the camera's textured black body.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two stacked lenses, the 'Yashica-Mat' and 'LM' branding, and the main lens assembly.
description_view_2: Opposite side view featuring a large knurled knob, a secondary dial, and a smooth black surface.
description_view_3: Rear or back view displaying a plain, flat black panel with minimal features and no visible controls.
